questions.q: What are the two main sources of internal finance for a business?
Distinguish between loan and equity finance.
questions.answer: Loan finance is borrowed from outsiders, while equity finance is contributed by the owners themselves. Loan finance is secured and repayable with interest, whereas equity finance is unsecured and repayable on winding up of the firm.

questions.q: State any two advantages of retained earnings as a source of finance.
questions.answer: Less expensive, No need to repay the amount with interest.

questions.q: What is the difference between term loan and cash credit facility?
questions.answer: The term loan is a loan taken for a specific period of time, whereas a cash credit facility is a loan that can be withdrawn and repaid as and when required. The term loan is repayable in fixed installments, whereas the cash credit facility is repayable on demand. The interest rate on a term loan is usually lower than that of a cash credit facility.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a feature of debentures?
What is the main difference between a mortgage loan and a hypothecation of goods?
questions.answer: A mortgage loan involves the transfer of ownership of a property to the lender, whereas hypothecation of goods involves the creation of a charge on specific assets.

questions.q: What are the characteristics of equity shares?
questions.answer: Perpetual, Redeemable on winding up of the company, Bearing a fixed dividend, Transferable, Ownership shares

questions.q: Distinguish between a share and a debenture.
questions.answer: A share represents ownership in the company, whereas a debenture represents a loan from the debenture holders. A share is a long-term source of finance, whereas a debenture is a short-term source of finance. A share is transferable, whereas a debenture is not transferable.

questions.q: Which of the following is a feature of a public deposit?
Explain any two methods of raising short-term finance from outsiders.
questions.answer: The two methods of raising short-term finance from outsiders are (i) Bank overdraft and (ii) Hire purchase. Bank overdraft is a flexible loan that can be withdrawn and repaid as and when required. It is repayable on demand and is usually secured by the assets of the business. Hire purchase is a method of financing the purchase of assets by paying a part of the price at the time of purchase and the balance in installments. It is a form of credit that allows the buyer to use the asset before the full price is paid.
